questions.question: Write a letter to your friend about a recent cricket match you watched at Chepauk Stadium.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Yohann,

I am writing this letter to tell you about the most thrilling cricket match I watched at the M.A. Chidambaram Stadium in Chepauk last Saturday. I wish you had been there with me.

Appa got two tickets for the CSK match against Mumbai Indians, and I was over the moon when he told me. We reached the stadium an hour early, and the atmosphere was absolutely electrifying. The entire crowd was dressed in yellow, and the chants of "Whistle Podu" echoed through the stands before the match even began. When the CSK players walked out for the toss, the roar from fifty thousand fans gave me goosebumps.

The match itself was a nail-biter. Mumbai batted first and posted a strong total of 185 runs. At one point, CSK were struggling at 92 for 4, and I thought all hope was lost. But then came the most unbelievable partnership. The last five overs were pure magic. Every boundary sent the crowd into a frenzy, and when the winning six was hit off the last ball, the entire stadium erupted. Appa and I were jumping and screaming with joy. I even lost my voice from all the cheering.

After the match, we stopped at a small shop near the stadium for some hot bajjis and filter coffee. It was the perfect ending to a perfect day. I took a few photographs of the stadium which I will show you at school on Monday.

You must come with us for the next match. I will ask Appa to get an extra ticket.

Your loving friend,
Anirudh

questions.question: Write a letter to your grandmother inviting her to your school's Annual Day function.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Paatti,

I am writing this letter to invite you to our school's Annual Day celebration at Sishya School, Adyar. It is going to be a grand event this year, and it would mean the world to me if you could be there.

The Annual Day is on Saturday, the 25th of April, 2026. The programme begins at 4:30 PM in the school auditorium. There will be dance performances, a short play, music recitals and speeches by the Chief Guest. The best part is that I have been selected to perform a mridangam solo on stage. I have been practising a special composition in Adi talam for the past three weeks, and my music teacher says it sounds wonderful. I really want you to watch me perform.

After the cultural programme, there is a prize distribution ceremony. I am hoping to receive a certificate for academic improvement this year. Amma and Appa will also be there, so we can all sit together. Prahlad is coming too, and I know he would love to see you.

Appa said he can pick you up from the station if you take the morning train. You can stay with us for the whole weekend, and Amma is already planning to make your favourite meals. Nandi has been missing you too. He still sits near the door every time we mention your name.

Please do come, Paatti. It will make my performance even more special knowing you are in the audience.

Your loving grandson,
Anirudh

questions.question: Write a letter to your cousin describing your experience learning a new raga in music class.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Adeep,

I am writing this letter to share something exciting that happened in my music class this month. I have started learning a completely new raga on the mridangam, and the experience has been both challenging and wonderful.

My music teacher introduced us to Raga Hamsadhwani last week. It is a beautiful raga with only five notes in the ascending scale, but playing the rhythmic patterns that accompany compositions in this raga is surprisingly tricky. The first two days, my fingers kept slipping into the patterns of Raga Shankarabharanam, which I had been practising for months. My teacher smiled and said that this is the hardest part of learning a new raga. Your fingers have a memory of their own, and you have to patiently train them to follow a new path.

By the third day, something clicked. I started hearing the distinct mood of Hamsadhwani in my head, and my hands began to follow naturally. There is a particular korvai that my teacher composed for this raga, and when I finally played it correctly from beginning to end without a single mistake, I felt such a rush of satisfaction. It was like solving a very difficult maths problem after struggling with it for hours.

I practise for about forty-five minutes every evening after homework. Amma sometimes sits in the hall and listens, and she says the new raga sounds very soothing. Even Nandi lies down quietly near my practice room when I play, which Appa jokes is either appreciation or resignation.

I remember you mentioning that you wanted to learn an instrument. Have you thought about it more? Let us talk about it when you visit us during the holidays.

Your loving brother,
Anirudh

questions.question: Write a letter to your friend who moved to another city, sharing school memories.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Suvasana,

I am writing this letter because I have been thinking about you a lot lately, especially since the new term started at Sishya. School feels different without you, and I wanted to share some memories that made me smile this week.

Do you remember the time we got into trouble during the science practical because we accidentally added too much baking soda to the vinegar, and the mixture overflowed all over the lab bench? The look on our teacher's face was priceless, and we could not stop laughing even though we knew we would be scolded. I walked past that same lab bench yesterday and could not help grinning.

I also keep thinking about the lunch breaks we spent near the big banyan tree in the playground. You, Yohann and I would sit there and share our food. You always had the best sandwiches, and I would trade my Amma's thepla for them. Those simple moments were some of the happiest times at school. The tree is still there, but the spot feels empty now.

The inter-house cricket tournament happened last week, and our house won. I scored twenty-three runs in the final match and wished you were there to cheer from the boundary. Yohann said the same thing. We both agree that our little group of three is incomplete without you.

How is your new school? Have you made good friends there? I hope you are settling in well. Please write back and tell me everything. And whenever your family plans a trip to Chennai, you must come and visit us. Amma keeps asking about you.

Your loving friend,
Anirudh

questions.question: Write a letter to your younger brother encouraging him to take up a musical instrument.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Prahlad,

I am writing this letter because I have been wanting to talk to you about something important, and I thought putting it in a letter would help me explain it better.

I have noticed that whenever I practise the mridangam at home, you sit nearby and watch with great curiosity. Last week, you even tried tapping the rhythms on the dining table with your palms, and honestly, your sense of timing was quite impressive for someone who has never had a single lesson. That made me think that you might really enjoy learning a musical instrument.

Learning music has been one of the best things I have ever done. When I started playing the mridangam three years ago, I was terrible. My fingers were clumsy, and I could not keep a steady beat for more than ten seconds. But slowly, with regular practice, everything began to fall into place. Now, playing the mridangam is the part of my day I look forward to the most. It helps me focus, it calms my mind after a long day at school, and the feeling of mastering a new composition is something truly special.

You do not have to choose the mridangam. There are so many wonderful instruments you could try. The veena, the violin, the flute or even the keyboard. Perhaps you could attend a trial class at my music teacher's academy and see which instrument speaks to you. I would be happy to go with you and introduce you to my teacher.

The most important thing is to start. You are at the perfect age to begin learning, and a few years from now, you will thank yourself for it. Imagine the two of us performing together at a family gathering. Amma and Appa would be so proud.

Think about it, and let me know what you decide.

Your loving brother,
Anirudh

questions.question: Write a letter to the Municipal Commissioner about poor street lighting in your area.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Municipal Commissioner,
Chennai Corporation,
Ripon Building, Park Town,
Chennai - 600003.

Dear Sir,

Subject: Complaint regarding poor street lighting in Indira Nagar, Adyar

I am writing this letter to bring to your urgent attention the extremely poor condition of street lighting in Indira Nagar, Adyar, particularly along 22nd Cross Street and the adjoining lanes.

Out of the twelve street lights installed on our road, seven have not been functioning for over two months. The remaining five are so dim that they barely illuminate the footpath. The stretch between the Indira Nagar bus stop and the turn towards 24th Cross Street is in complete darkness after 7:00 PM. Despite multiple complaints lodged by the residents' welfare association through the Chennai Corporation helpline, no action has been taken so far.

The lack of proper lighting has created serious safety concerns for the residents. Women and elderly citizens are afraid to walk on the streets after sunset. School children who return home from tuition classes in the evening are at risk. Two incidents of chain snatching have already been reported in our area in the past month, and residents believe the darkness has emboldened the criminals. Motorists also face difficulty navigating the unlit roads, and a cyclist was injured last week after riding into an open drain that was invisible in the dark.

I earnestly request you to take immediate action to repair or replace the non-functioning street lights in our locality. Installing LED lights with timers would be an efficient long-term solution. The safety and well-being of the residents depend on this basic civic facility being restored without further delay.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: Write a letter to the Principal requesting permission to organize a music club in school.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Principal,
Sishya School,
Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

Dear Madam,

Subject: Request for permission to start a music club in school

I am writing this letter on behalf of a group of musically inclined students to request your kind permission to establish a music club at Sishya School.

Our school has many talented students who play instruments such as the mridangam, veena, violin, keyboard and guitar, as well as several gifted singers. However, there is currently no formal platform within the school where these students can practise together, learn from one another and showcase their abilities. A music club would fill this gap and add tremendous value to the cultural life of our school.

I would like to propose the following structure for the club. The club would meet every Friday during the activity period for forty-five minutes. Sessions would include ensemble practice, listening sessions to appreciate different genres of Indian classical and contemporary music, and preparation for school events and interschool competitions. We would organise one informal concert each term, open to all students and teachers, to present the work of the club members. I have spoken to our music teacher, who has graciously agreed to serve as the faculty advisor.

We already have a list of thirty students from Classes 7 to 10 who have expressed strong interest in joining. The club would help students develop discipline, teamwork and creativity while keeping our rich musical traditions alive. It would also strengthen our school's representation in interschool cultural competitions.

I kindly request you to grant us permission and allot a suitable room for our weekly meetings.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: Write a letter to the Editor of a newspaper about the importance of preserving heritage buildings in Chennai.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Editor,
The Hindu,
Kasturi Buildings, Anna Salai,
Chennai - 600002.

Dear Sir,

Subject: The urgent need to preserve heritage buildings in Chennai

I am writing this letter to draw the attention of the public and the concerned authorities to the alarming rate at which heritage buildings in Chennai are being neglected and demolished, through the esteemed columns of your newspaper.

Chennai is home to some of the finest examples of colonial, Indo-Saracenic and Dravidian architecture. Buildings such as the Ripon Building, the Senate House, the Victoria Public Hall and several historic structures in George Town and Mylapore tell the story of our city's rich past. However, many of these structures are crumbling due to years of neglect, while others are being razed to make way for modern apartments and commercial complexes.

During a recent school project, our class researched the heritage buildings in the Mylapore area. We were shocked to discover that at least four buildings listed in the heritage register have been partially or fully demolished in the last three years alone. The ones still standing have cracked walls, broken windows and roofs that leak during every monsoon. No restoration work has been carried out despite these buildings being officially listed as protected structures.

Preserving heritage buildings is not just about saving old walls. It is about protecting our identity, our history and our culture. Cities like Jaipur and Pondicherry have shown that heritage conservation and modern development can go hand in hand. I urge the Chennai Corporation and the Tamil Nadu Heritage Commission to take immediate steps to restore these buildings and enforce strict penalties against unauthorised demolitions.

I hope this letter brings the matter to public attention and prompts the authorities to act before it is too late.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: Write a letter to the Manager of a sports shop about a defective cricket bat you purchased.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Manager,
Sports World,
15, Rajiv Gandhi Salai,
Adyar, Chennai - 600020.

Dear Sir,

Subject: Complaint regarding a defective cricket bat purchased from your store

I am writing this letter to bring to your notice that a cricket bat I recently purchased from your store is defective, and to request a replacement or a full refund.

On 20th March, 2026, I visited your store and purchased an SG English Willow cricket bat (Invoice No. SW/2026/3847) for Rs 4,500. The bat was bought for the upcoming interschool cricket tournament at my school, Sishya School, Adyar. I was very excited about using it, as your salesperson had assured me of its excellent quality and durability.

However, within the very first practice session, I noticed a deep crack running along the edge of the bat. By the third session, the crack had widened considerably, and a chunk of willow near the toe had begun to splinter. I had used the bat only on standard leather cricket balls and had followed all the recommended knock-in procedures. The damage is clearly due to a manufacturing defect and not misuse.

I am very disappointed, as I had specifically chosen your store based on its reputation for quality sports equipment. The interschool tournament is in two weeks, and I need a reliable bat urgently. I request you to either replace the defective bat with a new one of the same model or issue a full refund at the earliest.

I have enclosed a copy of the purchase receipt and photographs of the damaged bat for your reference.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: Write a letter to the Director of a museum requesting a guided tour for your class.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Director,
Government Museum,
Pantheon Road, Egmore,
Chennai - 600008.

Dear Sir,

Subject: Request for a guided tour of the Government Museum for school students

I am writing this letter on behalf of the students of Class 8C, Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai, to request you to arrange a guided tour of the Government Museum for our class.

We are currently studying the Mughal Empire and the Delhi Sultanate in our History and Civics course, and our teacher has recommended a visit to the museum to help us understand the artefacts and artwork from these periods. A guided tour would greatly enhance our understanding of the historical concepts we are learning in class and bring our textbooks to life.

Our group would consist of approximately forty students and three accompanying teachers. We would like to visit on a Saturday in the third week of April, 2026, preferably between 10:00 AM and 1:00 PM. We are particularly interested in the following galleries: the Bronze Gallery, the Archaeology Section, the Numismatics Gallery and the Arms and Armour display.

I kindly request you to provide us with the following information. First, the procedure for booking a guided tour for a school group. Second, the entry fee per student and whether any concession is available for school groups. Third, whether the museum provides a trained guide, or if we need to arrange one separately. Fourth, any rules regarding photography inside the galleries, as we would like to take photographs for our school project.

We assure you that the students will maintain discipline throughout the visit and follow all the rules and regulations of the museum.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: Write an email to a music academy enquiring about advanced mridangam classes.
questions.answer:
From: anirudh.ramkrishnan@sishya.edu.in
To: info@chennaimusicacademy.com
Subject: Enquiry about advanced mridangam classes

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this email finds you well. I am Anirudh Ramkumar, a Class 8 student at Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai. I have been learning the mridangam for the past three years under a private tutor, and I am now looking to join a reputed academy for advanced training. I came across your institution's name through a recommendation and am writing to enquire about the courses you offer.

I have completed learning the basic lessons including the arudhi, the standard korvais in Adi and Rupaka talams, and accompaniment for simple kritis. I am comfortable playing at moderate speeds and have performed at a few school events and family functions. I would now like to advance to concert-level accompaniment and learn more complex patterns.

Kindly provide me with the following details:
1. Whether your academy offers an advanced or intermediate-level mridangam course for students with three years of experience.
2. The class schedule, including the days, timings and duration of each session.
3. The monthly or term fees for the course.
4. Whether there is an audition or assessment before admission to determine the appropriate level.
5. The qualifications and experience of the mridangam teachers at your academy.

I would also like to know if weekend classes are available, as my school hours on weekdays make it difficult to attend sessions before 5:00 PM.

Looking forward to your reply.

Yours sincerely,
Anirudh Ramkumar
Class 8C, Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai

questions.question: Write an email to a bookshop owner ordering books for the school library.
questions.answer:
From: anirudh.ramkrishnan@sishya.edu.in
To: orders@higginbothams.com
Subject: Order for books for Sishya School library

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this email finds you well. I am Anirudh Ramkumar, the library monitor of Class 8C at Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai. Our school librarian has asked me to place an order for new books to be added to our school library for the current academic year.

We would like to order the following books:
1. "Wings of Fire" by A.P.J. Abdul Kalam - 3 copies
2. "The Story of My Experiments with Truth" by Mahatma Gandhi - 2 copies
3. "Treasure Island" by Robert Louis Stevenson - 3 copies
4. "A Brief History of Time" by Stephen Hawking - 2 copies
5. "The Diary of a Young Girl" by Anne Frank - 3 copies
6. "Malgudi Days" by R.K. Narayan - 3 copies
7. "The Jungle Book" by Rudyard Kipling - 2 copies
8. "India After Gandhi" by Ramachandra Guha - 2 copies

The total order is for 20 books. Kindly confirm the availability of all the titles and provide us with the total cost, including any bulk discount applicable for institutional orders. We would prefer the latest editions in paperback format.

Please also let us know the expected delivery timeline and whether free delivery is available for orders within Chennai. The books should be delivered to the school address: Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai - 600020, attention: School Librarian.

Payment will be made through a school cheque upon confirmation of the order.

Looking forward to your reply.

Yours sincerely,
Anirudh Ramkumar
Library Monitor, Class 8C
Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai

questions.question: Write an email to a resort manager booking rooms for a school trip to Pondicherry.
questions.answer:
From: anirudh.ramkrishnan@sishya.edu.in
To: reservations@pondicherrybeachresort.com
Subject: Room reservation for school trip from Sishya School, Chennai

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this email finds you well. I am Anirudh Ramkumar, a student of Class 8C at Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai. I am writing on behalf of our class to enquire about room availability and to make a reservation at your resort for our upcoming school trip to Pondicherry.

We are planning a two-night, three-day trip from Friday, 15th May to Sunday, 17th May, 2026. Our group will consist of approximately forty-two students and four teachers. We would require the following arrangements:
1. Ten rooms with quad sharing for the students (four students per room).
2. Two rooms with twin sharing for the teachers.
3. Breakfast, lunch and dinner for all three days, preferably both vegetarian and non-vegetarian options.
4. A conference hall or large room for one evening where we can conduct group activities and presentations.

We would also like to know whether your resort organises any sightseeing packages to popular Pondicherry attractions such as the Promenade Beach, Auroville, the French Quarter and Paradise Beach. If a package with transport is available, kindly include the details and pricing.

Please share the room tariff per night, the meal charges per person and any special discounts available for school groups. We would appreciate a detailed quotation so that we can seek approval from our school administration.

Looking forward to your reply.

Yours sincerely,
Anirudh Ramkumar
Class 8C, Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai

questions.question: Write an email to a guest speaker inviting them for your school's Science Day.
questions.answer:
From: anirudh.ramkrishnan@sishya.edu.in
To: dr.meenakshi.sundaram@iitm.ac.in
Subject: Invitation to be the guest speaker at Sishya School's Science Day

Dear Ma'am,

I hope this email finds you well. I am Anirudh Ramkumar, a student of Class 8C at Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai. On behalf of the Science Club and the school administration, I am writing to cordially invite you to be the guest speaker at our annual Science Day celebration.

Sishya School's Science Day is scheduled for Saturday, 9th May, 2026. The event will be held in our school auditorium from 9:30 AM to 1:00 PM. We are expecting an audience of approximately three hundred students from Classes 6 to 10, along with teachers and parents. The theme for this year's Science Day is "Science for a Sustainable Future," and we believe your expertise in environmental science and renewable energy would make your address truly inspiring for our students.

The programme details are as follows:
1. The guest speaker address is scheduled from 10:00 AM to 10:45 AM, followed by a brief question-and-answer session of fifteen minutes.
2. After the address, there will be a science exhibition where students will present their projects on topics related to sustainability.
3. We would be honoured if you could also spend a few minutes visiting the exhibition and interacting with the students.

Our school is located on the Adyar-Besant Nagar road, and we will arrange for transportation from your residence or office if needed. A small token of appreciation will be presented to you on behalf of the school.

We would be deeply honoured by your presence and guidance.

Looking forward to your reply.

Yours sincerely,
Anirudh Ramkumar
Class 8C, Sishya School, Adyar, Chennai

questions.question: Write an email to customer support about a delayed online order.
questions.answer:
From: anirudh.ramkrishnan@gmail.com
To: support@quickkart.in
Subject: Complaint regarding delayed delivery of Order No. QK-2026-78452

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to express my concern regarding the delayed delivery of an order I placed on your website ten days ago. Despite the promised delivery date having passed, I have neither received the package nor been given any satisfactory explanation for the delay.

The details of my order are as follows:
1. Order Number: QK-2026-78452
2. Date of Order: 22nd March, 2026
3. Items Ordered: A set of Faber-Castell colour pencils (48 shades) and a Classmate drawing book (A3 size)
4. Promised Delivery Date: 27th March, 2026
5. Delivery Address: 16/8, 22nd Cross Street, Indira Nagar, Adyar, Chennai - 600020

It has now been six days past the promised delivery date, and the tracking page on your website still shows the status as "In Transit" with no further updates since 25th March. I called your customer care helpline twice, on 28th March and 1st April, and was told both times that the issue would be resolved within twenty-four hours. However, no action has been taken.

I ordered these items specifically for a school art project that is due next week, and the delay is causing me significant inconvenience. I request you to either deliver the order within the next two days or issue a full refund to my account immediately.

Looking forward to your prompt response and resolution.

Yours sincerely,
Anirudh Ramkumar
16/8, 22nd Cross Street, Indira Nagar, Adyar, Chennai - 600020
